accurate.why: All claims match the bill text and verified context; the amendment, statute cited, and enactment date are accurate.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the five-year extension and statutory citation are correct and faithful to the text.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the bill extends funding authorization for monitoring, assessing, and researching the Great Lakes Basin.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly names section 201(d) of title II of division P of the Further Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2020, and describes the striking/inserting amendment.

exceptions_conditions.why: The bill is a simple date-change reauthorization with no material exceptions or carve-outs.

effective_dates.why: Notes the change took effect upon enactment on December 26, 2025, matching the bill.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the sole operative effect—extending the authorization end date from 2025 to 2030.

objective_tone.why: Neutral and factual with no advocacy or editorializing.

coherent.why: Well-organized, internally consistent, and clearly written.

concise.why: Succinct with minimal repetition and no filler.

correct_entities.why: The statute and bill title are named correctly; no entities are misidentified.

correct_figures.why: The years 2025/2030, five-year extension, and enactment date are all stated correctly.
